title: Nav Header
description: A horizontal navigation header that spans the top of the application. Contains the logo, page-title indicator, primary navigation (search), and user actions (My Work, account).
whenToUse: At the top of every authenticated page in the app shell. To house the brand mark, current page title, and primary global actions.
whenNotToUse: On marketing or landing pages (use a simpler header). On mobile-only views, consider a collapsible pattern.
acceptanceCriteria: Inner content uses a 3-column grid (left, center, right) constrained to max-width 1440px.
Icon-only ghost buttons in the account pill have aria-label.
My Work badge announces meaning (e.g. "N unread") to screen readers, not just the bare number.
When showSearch=false the centered search field is omitted and the layout remains balanced.
When showMyWork=false the My Work pill is omitted from the right region.
When titleOnly=true the title-area renders as a bare paragraph/xl-bold page title with no pill chrome (no icon, no chevron, no pill background, no shadow).

props.name: showSearch
props.description: When true, renders the centered search field. Mirrors the Figma `Show Search Box` boolean on the udc-nav-header component set.

props.name: showMyWork
props.description: When true, renders the My Work pill (with notification badge) in the right region. Mirrors the Figma `Show My Work` boolean on the udc-nav-header component set.

props.name: titleOnly
props.description: When true, the title-area renders as a bare page title (paragraph/xl-bold, no leading icon, no chevron, transparent pill chrome, radius 8). Markup: apply `data-title-only="true"` to `.udc-nav-title-area`. Mirrors the Figma `Title Only=True` variant on the `_udc-nav-header_title-area` set (5586:8859).

slots.name: brand
slots.description: Logo / brand mark on the far left. Figma binds this to surface/brand-bold. Figma source: `_udc-nav-header_logo` (6403:39131) with `Outline: False | True` variants.

slots.name: title-area
slots.description: Page-title pill displaying icon + page title + chevron, OR a bare page title (Title Only=True). CSS: `.udc-nav-title-area`. Figma source: `_udc-nav-header_title-area` (5586:8858).

slots.name: center
slots.description: Centered region — by default a search field. Hidden when showSearch=false.

slots.name: actions
slots.description: Right-side primary actions. By default the My Work pill; hidden when showMyWork=false.

slots.name: account
slots.description: Right-side account-actions pill containing three icon-only ghost buttons.

states.name: default
states.description: Default rendering of the header at full width.

states.name: title-only
states.description: Title-area renders as a bare paragraph/xl-bold page title with no pill chrome. Toggled via `data-title-only="true"` on `.udc-nav-title-area`. Mirrors Figma `Title Only=True` (5586:8859).

states.name: not-applicable
states.description: Figma does not define hover / focus / disabled states at the header level. Those states are inherited from nested components (search, button-ghost, badge).

keyboard.action: Move focus through nav header items

screenReader.announcement: Outer container exposes role="banner" so the header is identified as a landmark.

screenReader.trigger: Focus moves to an icon-only button
screenReader.announcement: Each icon-only ghost button in the account pill exposes an aria-label describing its action.
Badge count is exposed via accessible name (e.g. "My Work, 5 unread") rather than a bare number.

wcag.howMet: Semantic <header> + landmark role; account actions are real buttons.
Text and meaningful icon contrast meets 4.5:1 (text) / 3:1 (non-text). Currently unverified — see knownIssues for divergences between Figma intent and CSS.
All interactive elements are keyboard-reachable in DOM order.
Visible focus indicators on all focusable elements (account pill icon-only buttons inherit from button-ghost).
Icon-only ghost buttons require aria-label; My Work badge exposes accessible-name count.

knownIssues: Authoring errors ("Component set for node has existing errors") originate from the udc-badge component set (5794:7948) on the badge Figma page. They surface during deep inspection of nav-header because the My Work pill nests a udc-badge instance. The canonical udc-nav-header set (5684:11340) itself reads cleanly. The 🔴 blocked status on the nav-header page persists until that upstream Figma issue is resolved on the badge page.

Logo glyph in examples/default.html is an `apartment` Material Symbol; Figma's canonical logo is a hand-drawn brand-mark vector in `_udc-nav-header_logo` (6403:39131) with `Outline: False | True` variants (False = surface/brand-bold #023674; True = light fill). Replicating the exact vector requires an SVG export from Figma, and the `surface/brand-bold` color is in a Figma collection that is not currently imported into the doc-site's semantic tokens. The doc-site falls back to a Material Symbol with --uds-color-icon-interactive until both gaps are closed.

Logo `Outline=True` variant (light-fill brand vector at id 6403:39132) is defined in Figma but not exposed in docs. No prop to switch the logo to the inverse fill for use on dark surfaces; adding it requires a doc-side prop + a second SVG export. Deferred pending designer direction on whether this is a public contract or an internal-only variant.

New `_udc-nav-header_logo-pill` component exists in Figma as a 98×48 pill containing the brand vector + 'ResMan' text label (id 6399:13744). NOT wired into the canonical udc-nav-header Variant=Default tree — the canonical set still nests the simple 24×44 `_udc-nav-header_logo`. Treating as a design exploration; will not change the doc-side `.udc-nav-logo` chrome until the designer confirms whether the pill replaces the simple logo or coexists as a variant.

Frame 31 max-width (1440) is bound in Figma to `content-container/max-width-nav-container` — a Figma collection that is NOT currently in the imported token collections. CSS bakes 1440 as a literal until the collection is added to the token import scope.

Title-area chevron is decorative. The Figma `_udc-nav-header_title-area` Title Only=False variant renders a chevron next to the page title, but no popover/dropdown is wired into the canonical udc-nav-header tree (the prior bento app-switcher was removed in this round). The chevron currently serves as a visual affordance only; no interaction is documented in the canonical contract.

Standalone `_udc-nav-header_dropdown` (6019:32094) and `_udc-nav-header_nav-tile` (5498:2340) components still exist on the nav-header Figma page as orphans (not nested in any canonical component set). The bento app-switcher concept was removed from the canonical udc-nav-header but the sub-components were not yet deleted on the page. No doc-side surface for these orphans.
_legacyAccessibilityNotes: Use <header role="banner"> as the outer element. Icon-only ghost buttons in the account pill require aria-label.
